name: Mock Lobster Flemish Style
recipeIngredient: 1 Bottle beer (use Duvel, if you can afford it)
1 small Celery stalk with top, cut into chunks
recipeInstructions:
The main ingredient in this dish is monk fish, which has a taste and texture similar to lobster. Served in scallop shells or ramekins, it makes a delectable fish course during a multiple-course dinner.
1. Cut the fillets in half lengthwise, then cut each section into 3/4 inch slices.
2. In a large saucepan, place beer, water, onion, celery, salt, and thyme.
3. Heat to boiling.
4. Add fish, cover, and simmer for 4 minutes, or until the fish flakes.
5. Remove fish with a slotted spoon. Drain well on paper towels.
6. Boil stock for 10 minutes to reduce. Strain.
7. In another saucepan, melt butter. Stir in flour.
8. Add strained stock and cream. Cook, stirring frequently, until thickened.
9. Add a small amount of hot mixture to egg yolk and return to pan.
10. Cook slowly, stirring, for 1-2 minutes. Remove from heat and adjust seasonings.
11. Gently combine fish and sauce. Spoon into scallop shells or individual ramekins.
12. Sprinkle with cheese. Broil for 2 minutes, or until tops are lightly browned. Makes 6 appetizer servings.
